# Task 02.11: Regression Gates and Quality Reporting

## Sprint Info
- **Duration:** 4 days
- **Milestone:** M1.5 - Search Quality Evaluation Baseline
- **Priority:** P0
- **Depends On:** Tasks 02.7, 02.8, 02.9, 02.10
- **Blocks:** Tasks 05.1, 08.1, 09.3, 10.1

## What You'll Build
Create the quality gate that every future retrieval, ranking, spam, semantic, and freshness change must pass before being marked complete.

## Daily Breakdown

### Day 1: Gate Policy
- [ ] Define which metrics are blocking, warning-only, and informational.
- [ ] Define stricter gates for Persian/Iran-local queries.
- [ ] Define exceptions and review approval process.

### Day 2: Report Template
- [ ] Create a standard evaluation report template.
- [ ] Include baseline comparison, metric delta, latency impact, failure examples, and rollback notes.
- [ ] Include per-bucket Persian/Iran and freshness sections.

### Day 3: Task Definition Integration
- [ ] Update task templates and guide docs to require evaluation artifacts for quality-impacting changes.
- [ ] Define which task families are quality-impacting: retrieval, semantic, intent, spam, ranking, query pipeline, crawl/freshness.

### Day 4: Review Drill
- [ ] Run one dry review using the M1 baseline.
- [ ] Record reviewer questions and update the checklist.
- [ ] Publish the first quality report artifact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every quality-impacting task requires an evaluation artifact.
- [ ] Blocking regression thresholds are documented.
- [ ] Persian/Iran-local quality cannot regress silently.
- [ ] Rollback notes are required for deployable ranking/model changes.

## Required Evidence
- Quality gate policy.
- Evaluation report template.
- First dry-run quality report.
- Updated task-template checklist.
